Add personal superpowers overlay system

Enables users to write and manage their own skills alongside core skills.

## Key Features:
- Auto-setup on first session: Creates ~/.config/superpowers/ git repo
- Two-tier skills: Personal skills shadow core skills when paths match
- Environment variable support: PERSONAL_SUPERPOWERS_DIR, XDG_CONFIG_HOME
- GitHub integration: Optional public repo creation for sharing skills
- CLI-agnostic: Works across Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI (future)

## Changes:
- Added hooks/setup-personal-superpowers.sh - Auto-initializes personal repo
- Updated hooks/session-start.sh - Runs setup, offers GitHub repo creation
- Updated list-skills, skills-search - Search both personal and core skills
- Renamed skills/meta/creating-skills → writing-skills
- Added skills/meta/setting-up-personal-superpowers - Setup documentation
- Added skills/meta/sharing-skills - Contribution workflow
- Removed skills/meta/installing-skills - Old ~/.clank system
- Removed all INDEX.md files - Replaced by list-skills tool
- Updated README.md, getting-started - Document personal skills workflow

## Architecture:
~/.config/superpowers/skills/  # Personal (user-created, git-tracked)
${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/  # Core (read-only, from plugin)

Search order: Personal first, core second (first match wins)

All skills pressure-tested with subagents using RED-GREEN-REFACTOR methodology.
Add link to blog post introduction

Add general principle: specific instructions ≠ permission to skip workflows

Agents were interpreting "Add X to Y" as implicit permission to skip
brainstorming, TDD, or RED-GREEN-REFACTOR. Added general principle that
applies to ALL workflows:

"Your human partner's specific instructions describe WHAT to do, not HOW."

Then specific STOP for skill editing with required check-in.

Pressure tested and confirmed - agents now stop and check in before editing skills.

Succinct version (general principle ~5 lines + skill-specific ~12 lines) more
maintainable than repeating pattern in every workflow.

Add persuasion research foundation and enforce TDD for skill edits

Based on Meincke et al. (2025) research showing LLMs respond to same persuasion
principles as humans. Created reference doc explaining psychology behind effective
skill design (authority, commitment, scarcity, social proof, unity).

Extended Iron Law to explicitly cover skill edits - agents were skipping
RED-GREEN-REFACTOR when editing existing skills ("just a simple addition").

Changes:
- Add persuasion-principles.md reference doc with research citations
- Update creating-skills when_to_use to include editing skills
- Expand Iron Law with "Edit skill without testing? Same violation"
- Add explicit no-exceptions for "simple additions", "documentation updates"
- Add psychology note reference in bulletproofing section
- Add persuasion psychology section to testing-skills-with-subagents

Baseline testing showed agents already apply persuasion principles intuitively
when creating skills. This change makes the WHY explicit and prevents
rationalization that edits don't need testing.

Fix skills auto-update to fetch from tracking branch

Determine and fetch from the current branch's tracking remote instead of
hardcoding upstream/origin. This ensures the correct remote is updated
regardless of fork/upstream setup.

Apply Strunk's style principles to prose

Following The Elements of Style:
- Use active voice (Rule 10): "We rewrote" not "has been rewritten"
- Omit needless words (Rule 13): removed "automatically", "optionally", etc.
- Break up long sentences: split run-on second sentence into short, direct statements
- Put statements in positive form (Rule 11): direct assertions rather than negatives
- Keep it concise: "Claude notifies" not "Claude will notify"

commit.message:
Add git deployment steps to writing-skills checklist

Personal skills should be version controlled. Added deployment checklist items:
- Commit skill to git
- Push to GitHub
- Consider sharing to core

This ensures personal skills are properly tracked and backed up.

Inject skill tool paths via SessionStart hook

The SessionStart hook now tells Claude the exact paths to find-skills
and skill-run tools. Since hooks have CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT expanded,
the actual paths are injected into Claude's context.

This solves the problem of CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT not being available
as a bash environment variable.

Use explicit paths instead of CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT in bash code

CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is a template variable for documentation, not a bash
environment variable. Updated getting-started to use explicit paths that
work in actual bash commands.

Changes:
- Use ~/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers explicitly in bash examples
- Add helper to find plugin location if needed
- Update skill reading instructions to check both personal and core locations
Update skill-run documentation and examples

Remove old list-skills and skills-search tools

Consolidated into scripts/find-skills. The old tools in skills/getting-started/
are no longer needed - all functionality is now in scripts/find-skills which:
- Shows all skills with descriptions (default)
- Filters by pattern (when pattern provided)
- Searches both personal and core skills
- Works without CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT environment variable

Add unified scripts system with find-skills and run

Consolidates skill discovery and adds generic runner for cross-platform compatibility.

Changes:
- Created scripts/find-skills: Unified tool (show all + filter by pattern)
  - Shows descriptions by default
  - Searches personal first, then core (shadowing)
  - Logs searches for gap analysis
  - Bash 3.2 compatible

- Created scripts/run: Generic runner for any skill script
  - Searches personal superpowers first, then core
  - Enables running arbitrary skill scripts without CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var
  - Example: scripts/run skills/collaboration/remembering-conversations/tool/search-conversations

- Fixed bash 3.2 compatibility in list-skills, skills-search
  - Replaced associative arrays with newline-delimited lists
  - Works on macOS default bash (3.2) and Linux bash 4+

- Updated all documentation to reference scripts/find-skills
- Removed redundant wrapper scripts

This solves the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT environment variable issue - scripts
can now be called from anywhere without needing the env var set.

Allows indexing conversations without AI summary generation.

Mode-switching was overcomplicated. Back to phases with explicit permission

Simpler, clearer, preserves what worked.

Migration tested with 5,017 conversations and 6,385 exchanges.

Updated plugin source format to use URL instead of GitHub repo because the github repo format breaks for users without github ssh keys configured.

Fixes obra/superpowers#1

Bump version to 2.0.2

Bug fix release:

Fix false warning when local skills repo is ahead of upstream

The initialization script was incorrectly warning about upstream updates
when the local repository was ahead of upstream. This happened because
the logic didn't distinguish between three states:
- Local behind: LOCAL=BASE, REMOTE≠BASE → should update or warn
- Local ahead: LOCAL≠BASE, REMOTE=BASE → no warning needed
- Diverged: LOCAL≠BASE, REMOTE≠BASE → should warn

Changed the else clause to only set SKILLS_BEHIND when REMOTE≠BASE,
which correctly handles all three scenarios.

Fixes the issue reported in #8 where users with local commits ahead
of upstream were seeing spurious "new skills available" warnings.

Add DO NOT INDEX marker to summarization conversations

Prevents meta-conversations from polluting the search index.

Implementation:
- Marker added to summarizer system prompt
- All summarization conversations (chunk + synthesis) auto-excluded
- Conversations are still archived but not indexed
- Keeps search index focused on actual work conversations

Benefits:
- Automatic exclusion of meta-conversations
- No manual exclude list maintenance
- Works for both hierarchical and direct summarization
- Self-documenting in conversation logs

Tests: 67/67 passing including marker detection test

Add DO NOT INDEX marker to skip meta-conversations

Implements conversation-level exclusion via marker in content.

Feature:
- Conversations containing the marker are copied but not indexed
- Prevents pollution of search index with tool invocations
- Use in subagent prompts: <INSTRUCTIONS-TO-EPISODIC-MEMORY>DO NOT INDEX THIS CHAT</INSTRUCTIONS-TO-EPISODIC-MEMORY>

Benefits over path-based exclusions:
- Self-documenting - conversation declares its own exclusion
- Works for any use case (summarization, meta-work, sensitive content)
- No maintenance of exclusion lists

Test coverage: Verifies marked conversation is copied but not indexed

Add sync command for session-end hook integration

Implements episodic-memory sync to copy and index new conversations atomically.

Features:
- Copies .jsonl files from ~/.claude/projects to archive
- Only copies new or modified files (mtime check)
- Generates embeddings and indexes copied files
- Idempotent - safe to run repeatedly
- No explicit locking - uses atomic operations + SQLite WAL

Race condition handling:
- Atomic file copy (temp + rename)
- SQLite WAL mode for concurrent database writes
- mtime checks prevent duplicate copies
- last_indexed prevents duplicate indexing

Designed for Claude Code session-end hooks to automatically index
conversations without blocking or causing conflicts.

CLI tools (vim, menuconfig, REPLs, git rebase -i) through tmux sessions.

This enables Claude Code to automate traditionally manual workflows by:
- Creating detached tmux sessions
- Sending keystrokes programmatically
- Capturing terminal output

Includes:
- Plugin manifest and structure
- Complete skill documentation
- tmux-wrapper.sh helper script
- MIT license and README

commit.message:
fix: resolve symlinks in credential file mounts

Fixes issue where symlinked dotfiles (like ~/.gitconfig -> ~/dotfiles/.gitconfig)
would fail to mount properly in containers because the symlink target path
doesn't exist in the container filesystem.

## Changes

### Core Fix
- Add resolveMountPath() function to resolve symlinks before mounting
- Apply to .gitconfig and .npmrc file mounts
- Graceful fallback to original path if symlink resolution fails

### Testing
- TDD implementation with failing tests first
- Test symlinked files resolve to actual target paths
- Test regular files are unchanged
- Integration test with real user's symlinked ~/.gitconfig

### Error Handling
- Verbose warning if symlink resolution fails
- Safe fallback to original path maintains existing behavior
- No breaking changes for users without symlinked dotfiles

## Use Case

Common dotfile management pattern:
```bash
~/.gitconfig -> ~/dotfiles/.gitconfig  # symlink
```

Before: Mount fails because ~/dotfiles/ doesn't exist in container
After: Mounts actual file ~/dotfiles/.gitconfig directly

Verified with real-world symlinked dotfile setup.

Implements -p/--publish flag for publishing container ports to host:

## Features Added

### CLI Interface
- `-p/--publish` flag with Docker-compatible syntax
- Support for multiple port mappings: `-p 8080:3000 -p 9000:9001`
- All Docker port formats supported:
  - Basic: `8080:3000`
  - Host IP: `127.0.0.1:8080:3000`
  - Protocol: `8080:3000/tcp`, `5353:53/udp`
  - Combined: `127.0.0.1:8080:3000/tcp`

### Implementation
- Added PublishPorts field to RunConfig
- Port mappings passed directly to docker run command
- Comprehensive test coverage with TDD approach

### Testing
- Flag parsing tests for all Docker formats
- Multiple port mapping tests
- Integration tests for RunConfig flow
- End-to-end verification

## Usage
```bash
packnplay run -p 8080:3000 npm start
packnplay run -p 127.0.0.1:8080:3000/tcp -p 5432:5432 npm dev
```

Zero learning curve - uses exact Docker syntax developers already know.

feat: implement smart user detection with caching and direct container interrogation

Replace hardcoded "vscode" user assumptions with intelligent runtime detection:

## Major Improvements

### Direct Detection (No More Guessing)
- Ask containers directly: `whoami && echo $HOME`
- Works with ANY Docker image (node, ubuntu, python, custom)
- Gets real runtime user + actual home directory
- Zero assumptions about image conventions

### Image-Based Caching
- Cache results by Docker image ID (not tag)
- XDG-compliant cache: `~/.cache/packnplay/userdetect/`
- Massive performance improvement for repeated use
- Atomic cache writes prevent corruption

### Updated Components
- pkg/userdetect: New detection engine with comprehensive tests
- pkg/devcontainer: Integrates detection, removes hardcoded defaults
- pkg/agents: Dynamic user paths for all 8 AI coding agents
- pkg/integration: End-to-end user detection verification

### Detection Priority
1. devcontainer.json remoteUser (explicit config)
2. Cache lookup (by image ID)
3. Direct container detection (whoami + $HOME)
4. Safe fallback (root)

## Before vs After

Before: Hardcoded "vscode" everywhere, breaks with other images
After: Universal compatibility with intelligent caching

Performance: 5+ container runs per detection → 1 run (first time only)
